posts.title: Introduction to Power Platform
posts.text: Introduction to Power PlatformIn this module, you will: Learn the components and features of Power Platform Identify when to use each Power Platform component application to create business solutions Learn the value of using the Power Platform to create business solutions What is Power Platform?Act by building custom apps in Power Apps, automate processes based on the data you collected in Power Automate, and analyze the data you have collected in Power BI.The power platform can add value to any business by helping you to analyze, act and automate. Power Apps build custom apps for business needs web and mobile applications Power Automate (used to named as Microsoft Flow) automated workflows between applications and services communication data collections decision approvals Power BI business analytics service insights leg of the Power Platform can potentially replace a standing meeting to report out on company metrics: sales data progress against goals employee performance Power Virtual Agents create powerful chatbots users can enable chatbots to perform an action by simply calling a Power Automate flow Features AI Builder workflows Power apps Common Data Service let users securely store and manage data from multiple sources and integrate that data foundation that enables the consolidation, display and manipulation of data A type of Tabular Data Connectors enable to connect apps, data and devices in the cloud bridge from data source to app, workflow and dashboard understand the types of data sources they connect Tabular Data (in a structured table foramt) Common Data Service SharePoint SQL Server Function-based data (a table of data but offer more extensive action such as the ability to send an email, update permissions or create a calendar event) Office 365 Users Project Online Azure Blob Storage types of connectors Standard SharePoint Outlook YouTube Premium (require additional licensing for your app and/or users) SQL Server Survey Money Mail Chimp users can also use APpSource to cource and install apps and use the connectors to non-Microsoft services Connectors can provide input and output between the data source and the Power Platform once estabilished a data source and configured concector, two types of operations: Triggers only used in Power Automate can be time based prompt a flow to begin Actions used in Power Automate and Power Apps be prompted by the user or a trigger and allow interaction with your data source by some function Custom Connectors call a public available API advantage: used in different platforms Power Apps Power Automate Azure Logic Apps Data loss prevention, compliance, privacy and accessibility data loss prevention policies connectors type in the context of the organization Business Non-Business (defaulted set) Blocked Data protection all public endpoints are secured using industry-standard TLS the Power Platform does fully support the GDPR (The European Union General Data Protection Regulation) Accessibility in the Power Platform an accessible canvas app will allow users with vision, hearing and other impairments to successfully use the app Summary and Key take aways Power Platform is a system that enables users to do three key actions on data that help them drive business: Analyze: gain insights from data Act: drive intelligent business processes via apps they build Automate: automate busness processes Power BI hleps you analyze and visualize data on a unified platform with data from internal and external sources Power Apps helps you build and deploy customized apps that work across web and mobile, embedded or standalone, on any device Connectors are brighes that allow you to send information from your data source to your app or workflow and back Power Automate helps you create automation workflows, from simple to advanced scenarios

posts.title: Melbourn Cup! Welcome on board!
posts.text: Day One get your badge from Michelle of Reception Meet and Greet with Nathan / Justin Meet your assigned onboarding Take a tour of the office. Find the toilets, pantry/ lunch room complete the online training from ASI Solution Meeting with Andrew PanIssue one： Scope of work page one: infomation of each school’s detail page two and following: information of each room the main issue is: we don’t need to create the room page one by one, but we can set the number of rooms in the school info page and generate the number of room page we want. In the room page, if the user pick Fibrous Cement, the section of uploading the image will pop up. SolutionConditions– true or false Issue two: Falcrum App PDF formatFalcrum App is similar to Power Apps change the format based on Justin’s requirements (print version) Logo does not fit in the foot Solutionmodify the html and cssSolve this tomorrow Meeting with Valerie Introduce ASI background Introduce the team Introduce the culture and mission of our company Introduce the company values Power Apps Docs learn about Introduction to Power Platform LogToday is my first day at ASI Solution. I was too nervous to fall asleep yesterday. I woke up at 7 am which is much earlier than I used to. I arrived at the company around 8:30, waiting for Huy at the cafe. My first day in ASI Solution is also a day of Melbourn Cup. The company has a big BBQ gathering outside of our company.I didn’t know that I am the only employee who has actually no background on Microsoft platform but work as a Microsoft Surface Specialist & Power Apps Developer. Justin prefers to hire someone who has 8 years working experience but Huy still decided to hire me because he said he can see the huge potential I possessed. I really appreciate this opportubity and I hope that I can contribute to the company as well.Huy led me to meet everyone in the company. “Welcome on board” is the most frequent statement I heared today. This is such a wonderful experience to work with folks from different backgrounds. Most of them are Aussies and sometimes I could feel the language barriers.Today, I feel sleepy because I could not fall asleep last night until 3 am. I met some intersting and friendly colleagues today, like Alex, Oscar, Andrew~ Hope that I can remember all the names of our company within one month~Tomorrow is another training day. Looking forward to it!

posts.title: Farewell, Julian
posts.text: Connie is my savior. I knew Connie in 2019 when I worked in Stomble with Garlio, who is Connie’s younger brother. He recommended me to his sister’s language center to teach English-speakers mandarin. Cathy interviewed me and I joined CCLingo language center successfully in June, 2019. Connie is the founder of CCL. At that moment, she was an employee at Microsoft. In retrospect, Connie is the answer to my prayer: she provided me a wonderful platform at CCLingo and this April she introduced me to Happy Angels Home to work as a disability support worker. I spent 6 months in total with Julian, who is an autism teenager living in Penshurst. Julian graduated from a special school in 2018, and then he started studying in a day program in Caringbah. It is a program designed for the disabled to study together after graduation. However, due to the pandamic, Julian’s previous day program suspended and he had to stay at home until the epidemic is over. That’s why I got this opportunity to work with Julian and spent such wonderful time with him. I still remember the first day I met Julian, he was quite ingoing, listening to the conversation between Katherine (Julian’s mom) and I. This was my first time contacting with children with autism. Julian is non-verbal and he also has some uncontrollable behaviour on a daily basis. I was kind of nervous on the first day, because I feel it was quiet exhausted to explain everything to him and I had no idea whether he can understand what I said or not. I supposed to teach Julian 3 to 4 hours at the beginning, but on the first day, I finished my teaching within 2 hours with a sense of frustration. It seems unbelievable that I feel enjoyable to teach Julian later on when I found the way to teach him. We did Reading Eggs together, which is a kind of Australian textbook for children from year 3 to year 13. I was astonished to see Julian’s progress day by day. He is amazing and I gained a sense of accomplishment when I tutored him gradually. I also teach him mathematics. To my surprised, he loves maths more than other subjects. Different from us, he is good at mental arithmetic. He has obsessive-compulsive disorder in study, for example, if he haven’t finished his homework, he won’t start doing the next tasks. I enjoyed the time staying with him. He showed me the potential he can be smarter. When I first talked with Julian, he can’t answer my questions but repeat my questions on the iPad. As I have mentioned, he can’t speak because he is a non-verbal. The only way to communicate is to use a iPad. He was accustomed to repeating questions over and over again rather than understand the question and answer it. It is quiet hard to practice this communication skills at the beginning, because he used to talk like a parrot and just repeat what he heard. However, since September, Julian can answer some simple questions using his communication tool without repeating questions like a parrot. Julian can play piano!! And he doesn’t need to look at the piano textbook while playing the song. I love Princess Waltz he played. I was surprised that he has such a wonderful gift, because I don’t know any tunes but he can read music. We also do some exercises at the yard in front of his house. He was a slim boy when he was young, but now he seems getting stronger and fatter than before. 😂 Julian’s favorite thing to do is to take the train! He loves to sit on smoothly moving objects like trains. I also noticed that he watched vlog related to train and played computer games related to train. Once I asked him, “Do you want to become a train driver?” He nodded. I took him to Miranda shopping center and he can remember the way to the train station. When he saw the train drive slowly in, he seemed excited. We drew pictures every Saturday, went shopping every Friday and really had a good time together! However, I have to say googbe to Julian’s family temporarily, because I will start my journey at ASI solution as a developer tomorrow. No one knows the way forward, but just work hard! Goodbye, Julian~I will miss you~ Julian’s painting Julian's painting-Animal Julian's painting-Flower Julian's painting-Plum

posts.title: 1. Binary_Gap
posts.text: Qeustion A binary gap within a positive integer N is any maximal sequence of consecutive zeros that is surrounded by ones at both ends in the binary representation of N.For example, number 9 has binary representation 1001 and contains a binary gap of length 2. The number 529 has binary representation 1000010001 and contains two binary gaps: one of length 4 and one of length 3. The number 20 has binary representation 10100 and contains one binary gap of length 1. The number 15 has binary representation 1111 and has no binary gaps. The number 32 has binary representation 100000 and has no binary gaps.Write a function:class Solution { public int solution(int N); }that, given a positive integer N, returns the length of its longest binary gap. The function should return 0 if N doesn’t contain a binary gap.For example, given N = 1041 the function should return 5, because N has binary representation 10000010001 and so its longest binary gap is of length 5. Given N = 32 the function should return 0, because N has binary representation ‘100000’ and thus no binary gaps.Write an efficient algorithm for the following assumptions:N is an integer within the range [1..2,147,483,647]. Solution use a list to store the index of each “1” calculate the maximum gapnote: need to omit distance == 1~ Python Codingdef solution(N): one_list = [] res = 0 bin_n = str(format(N, 'b')) # get the index list of each one for i in range(len(bin_n)): if bin_n[i] == '1': one_list.append(i) #calculate the largest gap between each index for j in range(len(one_list)-1): distance = one_list[j+1] - one_list[j] if len(one_list) == 1 and j == 0: return 0 elif distance == 1: pass else: if distance > res: res = distance -1 return max(res, 0) Result
